<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天文道]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天文道]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天文道]]
{{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmondō''' (天文道) refers to the ancient {{ill|study|en|study|WD=Q340383|lt=academic discipline}} of observing and recording [[astronomical phenomena]] and analyzing their effects on earthly affairs. It was one of the subjects taught at the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. While it corresponds to what we today call {{ill|Astronomy|en|Astronomy|WD=Q333|lt=astronomy}}, its content was heavily influenced by {{ill|Astrology|en|Astrology|WD=Q34362|lt=astrology}} and far removed from modern science.
== Tenmon == The term "'''tenmon'''" refers to the recording of changes observed in the heavens. The establishment of '''astronomy''' as a scientific discipline in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} began only after the introduction of Western astronomy during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}. Therefore, some astronomers familiar with the distinction between tenmon and astronomy during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}} proposed new terms, such as '''seigaku''' ("stellar studies"), to replace traditional terms like tenmon(dō).
On the other hand, {{ill|rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} (calendrical studies) is considered closer to modern {{ill|celestial mechanics|ja|暦算天文学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial mechanics|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦学|1=rekigaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514951}} and {{ill|Astrometry|en|Astrometry|WD=Q181505|lt=positional astronomy}}.
== Astronomical Anomalies == {{ill|Astronomical events|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomical events|qq=}} refer to rare or unusual celestial phenomena, such as {{ill|Solar eclipse|en|Solar eclipse|WD=Q3887|lt=solar eclipses}}, {{ill|Lunar eclipse|en|Lunar eclipse|WD=Q44235|lt=lunar eclipses}}, {{ill|Meteor|en|Meteor|WD=Q131136|lt=meteors}}, {{ill|Comet|en|Comet|WD=Q3559|lt=comets}}, unusual proximity between the {{ill|Moon|en|Moon|WD=Q405|lt=moon}} and {{ill|Astronomical object|en|Astronomical object|WD=Q6999|lt=stars}}, close conjunctions of {{ill|Planet|en|Planet|WD=Q634|lt=planets}}, {{ill|red auroras|ja|赤気|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/red auroras|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|halos|ja|光暈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/halos|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暈|1=halo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q186310}}, and {{ill|white rainbows|ja|白虹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/white rainbows|1=white rainbows|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580362}}.
These phenomena were believed to have profound implications for earthly affairs, especially the fate of states and their rulers (e.g., {{ill|King|en|King|WD=Q12097|lt=monarchs}}, {{ill|Emperor|en|Emperor|WD=Q39018|lt=emperors}}, or {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=the Japanese emperor}}). Predictable events were avoided on important occasions, while sudden phenomena were observed and interpreted through divination. These interpretations were reported to the ruler, often accompanied by countermeasures. The highest authority on Tenmondō in Japan, the {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, was responsible for formulating these responses, recording the details of the anomalies, and submitting them to the emperor through the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} or {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人|1=Kurōdo-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6447696}}. This was known as {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}}.
== Tenmondō == == Overview == Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}, Tenmondō, along with Rekidō and {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}}, fell under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. It was overseen by a {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (limited to one person with the Rank of {{ill|Jushichi-i Ge|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushichi-i Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}) and assisted by ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Tenmon phenomena were considered critical to the state's survival, and even Tenmonsei were forbidden to access related texts without authorization ({{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Ritsuryō}}, Zatsuryō - Hisoryō Genjō clause). Disclosure of observation results was also prohibited.
The Tenmon Hakase, along with the Tenmonsei, conducted nightly observations of the sky to detect anomalies and made Tenmon Missō reports when necessary. They also educated the Tenmonsei using texts such as the {{ill|Book of Han|en|Book of Han|WD=Q846905|lt=Book of Han}} and {{ill|Book of Jin|en|Book of Jin|WD=Q1154753|lt=Book of Jin}} (astronomy sections), {{ill|Tengen-sho|ja|天漢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengen-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sangakyohansan|ja|三家薄讃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangakyohansan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
However, compared to Onmyōdō, Tenmondō was not as highly regarded in the Japanese {{ill|Ritsuryō state|ja|律令国家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō state|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 律令制|1=ritsuryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q742460}}. Observations were often limited to fixed times (around {{ill|the hour of the dog|ja|戌の刻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/the hour of the dog|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戌|1=Dog|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q755126}} and {{ill|the hour of the tiger|ja|寅の刻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/the hour of the tiger|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寅|1=yín|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16683683}}).
=== Control by the Abe Clan === During the {{ill|mid-Heian period|ja|平安時代中期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mid-Heian period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Abe clan gained control of Tenmondō following the succession from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori}} to {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei}}. The discipline became part of the Abe clan's {{ill|family learning|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family learning|1=Family knowledge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455404}} (later forming the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}), and participation by outsiders was discouraged.
{{more about Abe clan influence}} === Transition to Modern Astronomy === The transition to modern {{ill|Astronomy|en|Astronomy|WD=Q333|lt=astronomy}} began with the establishment of the {{ill|Tenmongata|en|Tenmongata|WD=Q11442660|lt=Tenmonkata}} by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, remnants of Tenmondō's astrological elements persisted.
== References == * Yamashita, K. "The Formation and Development of the Kamo and Abe Clans" (in {{ill|Heian Religion and Onmyōdō|ja|平安時代の宗教文化と陰陽道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Religion and Onmyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). * Akazawa, H. "The Development of the Abe Clan during the Kamakura Period" (in {{ill|Kamakura Period Officials and Onmyōji|ja|鎌倉期官人陰陽師の研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Period Officials and Onmyōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). * Sato, H. "Tenmondō" (in {{ill|Heian Period Encyclopedia|ja|平安時代史事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Period Encyclopedia|1=Heian Jidaishi Jiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482399}}). * Mori, Y. "Tenmondō" (in {{ill|Japanese Historical Encyclopedia 4|ja|日本史大事典 4|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Encyclopedia 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). * Nakayama, S. Predictable events were avoided on important occasions, while sudden phenomena were observed and interpreted through divination. These interpretations were reported to the ruler, often accompanied by countermeasures. The highest authority on Tenmondō in Japan, the {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, was responsible for formulating these responses, recording the details of the anomalies, and submitting them to the emperor through the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} or {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人|1=Kurōdo-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6447696}}. This was known as {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}}.
== Tenmondō == == Overview == Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}, Tenmondō, along with Rekidō and {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}}, fell under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. It was overseen by a {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (limited to one person with the Rank of {{ill|Jushichi-i Ge|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushichi-i Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}) and assisted by ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Tenmon phenomena were considered critical to the state's survival, and even Tenmonsei were forbidden to access related texts without authorization ({{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Ritsuryō|1=Yōrō Code}}, Zatsuryō - Hisoryō Genjō clause). Disclosure of observation results was also prohibited.
The Tenmon Hakase, along with the Tenmonsei, conducted nightly observations of the sky to detect anomalies and made Tenmon Missō reports when necessary. They also educated the Tenmonsei using texts such as the {{ill|Book of Han|en|Book of Han|WD=Q846905|lt=Book of Han|1=Book of Han}} and {{ill|Book of Jin|en|Book of Jin|WD=Q1154753|lt=Book of Jin|1=Book of Jin}} (astronomy sections), {{ill|Tengen-sho|ja|天漢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengen-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sangakyohansan|ja|三家薄讃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangakyohansan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
However, compared to Onmyōdō, Tenmondō was not as highly regarded in the Japanese {{ill|Ritsuryō state|ja|律令国家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō state|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 律令制|1=Ritsuryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q742460}}. Observations were often limited to fixed times (around {{ill|the hour of the dog|ja|戌の刻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/the hour of the dog|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戌|1=Dog (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q755126}} and {{ill|the hour of the tiger|ja|寅の刻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/the hour of the tiger|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寅|1=yín|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16683683}}).
=== Control by the Abe Clan === During the {{ill|mid-Heian period|ja|平安時代中期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mid-Heian period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Abe clan gained control of Tenmondō following the succession from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori|1=Kamo no Yasunori}} to {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}}. The discipline became part of the Abe clan's {{ill|family learning|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family learning|1=Family knowledge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455404}} (later forming the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}), and participation by outsiders was discouraged.
{{more about Abe clan influence}} === Transition to Modern Astronomy === The transition to modern {{ill|Astronomy|en|Astronomy|WD=Q333|lt=astronomy|1=Astronomy}} began with the establishment of the {{ill|Tenmongata|en|Tenmongata|WD=Q11442660|lt=Tenmonkata|1=Tenmongata}} by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, remnants of Tenmondō's astrological elements persisted.

{{wikidata link|Q11442664}} [[Category:Astrology]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of astronomy in Japan]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天文道]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天文道]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天文道]]
{{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmondō''' (天文道) refers to the ancient {{ill|study|en|study|WD=Q340383|lt=academic discipline|1=Study}} of observing and recording [[astronomical phenomena]] and analyzing their effects on earthly affairs. It was one of the subjects taught at the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. While it corresponds to what we today call {{ill|Astronomy|en|Astronomy|WD=Q333|lt=astronomy|1=Astronomy}}, its content was heavily influenced by {{ill|Astrology|en|Astrology|WD=Q34362|lt=astrology|1=Astrology}} and far removed from modern science.
== Tenmon == The term "'''tenmon'''" refers to the recording of changes observed in the heavens. The establishment of '''astronomy''' as a scientific discipline in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} began only after the introduction of Western astronomy during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. Therefore, some astronomers familiar with the distinction between tenmon and astronomy during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}} proposed new terms, such as '''seigaku''' ("stellar studies"), to replace traditional terms like tenmon(dō).
On the other hand, {{ill|rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} (calendrical studies) is considered closer to modern {{ill|celestial mechanics|ja|暦算天文学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial mechanics|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦学|1=rekigaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514951}} and {{ill|Astrometry|en|Astrometry|WD=Q181505|lt=positional astronomy|1=Astrometry}}.
== Astronomical Anomalies == {{ill|Astronomical events|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomical events|qq=}} refer to rare or unusual celestial phenomena, such as {{ill|Solar eclipse|en|Solar eclipse|WD=Q3887|lt=solar eclipses|1=Solar eclipse}}, {{ill|Lunar eclipse|en|Lunar eclipse|WD=Q44235|lt=lunar eclipses|1=Lunar eclipse}}, {{ill|Meteor|en|Meteor|WD=Q131136|lt=meteors|1=Meteor}}, {{ill|Comet|en|Comet|WD=Q3559|lt=comets|1=Comet}}, unusual proximity between the {{ill|Moon|en|Moon|WD=Q405|lt=moon|1=Moon}} and {{ill|Astronomical object|en|Astronomical object|WD=Q6999|lt=stars|1=Astronomical object}}, close conjunctions of {{ill|Planet|en|Planet|WD=Q634|lt=planets|1=Planet}}, {{ill|red auroras|ja|赤気|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/red auroras|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|halos|ja|光暈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/halos|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暈|1=Halo (optical phenomenon)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q186310}}, and {{ill|white rainbows|ja|白虹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/white rainbows|1=Q11580362|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580362}}.
These phenomena were believed to have profound implications for earthly affairs, especially the fate of states and their rulers (e.g., {{ill|King|en|King|WD=Q12097|lt=monarchs|1=King}}, {{ill|Emperor|en|Emperor|WD=Q39018|lt=emperors|1=Emperor}}, or {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=the Japanese emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}}). Predictable events were avoided on important occasions, while sudden phenomena were observed and interpreted through divination. These interpretations were reported to the ruler, often accompanied by countermeasures. The highest authority on Tenmondō in Japan, the {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, was responsible for formulating these responses, recording the details of the anomalies, and submitting them to the emperor through the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} or {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人|1=Kurōdo-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6447696}}. This was known as {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}}.
== Tenmondō == == Overview == Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}, Tenmondō, along with Rekidō and {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}}, fell under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. It was overseen by a {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (limited to one person with the Rank of {{ill|Jushichi-i Ge|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushichi-i Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}) and assisted by ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Observations were often limited to fixed times (around {{ill|the hour of the dog|ja|戌の刻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/the hour of the dog|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戌|1=Dog (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q755126}} and {{ill|the hour of the tiger|ja|寅の刻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/the hour of the tiger|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寅|1=yín|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16683683}}).
=== Control by the Abe Clan === During the {{ill|mid-Heian period|ja|平安時代中期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mid-Heian period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Abe clan gained control of Tenmondō following the succession from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori|1=Kamo no Yasunori}} to {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}}. The discipline became part of the Abe clan's {{ill|family learning|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family learning|1=Family knowledge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455404}} (later forming the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}), and participation by outsiders was discouraged.

This was known as {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}}.
== Tenmondō == == Overview == Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}, Tenmondō, along with Rekidō and {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}}, fell under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. It was overseen by a {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (limited to one person with the Rank of {{ill|Jushichi-i Ge|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushichi-i Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}) and assisted by ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Tenmon phenomena were considered critical to the state's survival, and even Tenmonsei were forbidden to access related texts without authorization ({{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Ritsuryō|1=Yōrō Code}}, Zatsuryō - Hisoryō Genjō clause). Disclosure of observation results was also prohibited.
The Tenmon Hakase, along with the Tenmonsei, conducted nightly observations of the sky to detect anomalies and made Tenmon Missō reports when necessary. They also educated the Tenmonsei using texts such as the {{ill|Book of Han|en|Book of Han|WD=Q846905|lt=Book of Han|1=Book of Han}} and {{ill|Book of Jin|en|Book of Jin|WD=Q1154753|lt=Book of Jin|1=Book of Jin}} (astronomy sections), {{ill|Tengen-sho|ja|天漢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengen-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sangakyohansan|ja|三家薄讃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangakyohansan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
However, compared to Onmyōdō, Tenmondō was not as highly regarded in the Japanese {{ill|Ritsuryō state|ja|律令国家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō state|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 律令制|1=Ritsuryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q742460}}. Observations were often limited to fixed times (around {{ill|the hour of the dog|ja|戌の刻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/the hour of the dog|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戌|1=Dog (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q755126}} and {{ill|the hour of the tiger|ja|寅の刻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/the hour of the tiger|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寅|1=yín|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16683683}}).
=== Control by the Abe Clan === During the {{ill|mid-Heian period|ja|平安時代中期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mid-Heian period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Abe clan gained control of Tenmondō following the succession from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori|1=Kamo no Yasunori}} to {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}}. The discipline became part of the Abe clan's {{ill|family learning|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family learning|1=Family knowledge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455404}} (later forming the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}), and participation by outsiders was discouraged.
{{more about Abe clan influence}} === Transition to Modern Astronomy === The transition to modern {{ill|Astronomy|en|Astronomy|WD=Q333|lt=astronomy|1=Astronomy}} began with the establishment of the {{ill|Tenmongata|en|Tenmongata|WD=Q11442660|lt=Tenmonkata|1=Tenmongata}} by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, remnants of Tenmondō's astrological elements persisted.
== References == * Yamashita, K. "The Formation and Development of the Kamo and Abe Clans" (in {{ill|Heian Religion and Onmyōdō|ja|平安時代の宗教文化と陰陽道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Religion and Onmyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). * Akazawa, H. "The Development of the Abe Clan during the Kamakura Period" (in {{ill|Kamakura Period Officials and Onmyōji|ja|鎌倉期官人陰陽師の研究|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Period Officials and Onmyōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). * Sato, H. "Tenmondō" (in {{ill|Heian Period Encyclopedia|ja|平安時代史事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Period Encyclopedia|1=Heian Jidaishi Jiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482399}}). * Mori, Y. "Tenmondō" (in {{ill|Japanese Historical Encyclopedia 4|ja|日本史大事典 4|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Encyclopedia 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). * Nakayama, S. "Tenmondō" (in {{ill|National Historical Encyclopedia 9|ja|国史大辞典 9|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Historical Encyclopedia 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).

revision.text:
{{Refimprove|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmondō''' (天文道) refers to the ancient {{ill|study|en|study|WD=Q340383|lt=academic discipline|1=Study}} of observing and recording [[astronomical phenomena]] and analyzing their effects on earthly affairs. It was one of the subjects taught at the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. While it corresponds to what we today call {{ill|Astronomy|en|Astronomy|WD=Q333|lt=astronomy|1=Astronomy}}, its content was heavily influenced by {{ill|Astrology|en|Astrology|WD=Q34362|lt=astrology|1=Astrology}} and far removed from modern science.
== Tenmon == The term "'''tenmon'''" refers to the recording of changes observed in the heavens. The establishment of '''astronomy''' as a scientific discipline in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} began only after the introduction of Western astronomy during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. Therefore, some astronomers familiar with the distinction between tenmon and astronomy during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}} proposed new terms, such as '''seigaku''' ("stellar studies"), to replace traditional terms like tenmon(dō).
On the other hand, {{ill|rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} (calendrical studies) is considered closer to modern {{ill|celestial mechanics|ja|暦算天文学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/celestial mechanics|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦学|1=rekigaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514951}} and {{ill|Astrometry|en|Astrometry|WD=Q181505|lt=positional astronomy|1=Astrometry}}.
== Astronomical Anomalies == {{ill|Astronomical events|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomical events|qq=}} refer to rare or unusual celestial phenomena, such as {{ill|Solar eclipse|en|Solar eclipse|WD=Q3887|lt=solar eclipses|1=Solar eclipse}}, {{ill|Lunar eclipse|en|Lunar eclipse|WD=Q44235|lt=lunar eclipses|1=Lunar eclipse}}, {{ill|Meteor|en|Meteor|WD=Q131136|lt=meteors|1=Meteor}}, {{ill|Comet|en|Comet|WD=Q3559|lt=comets|1=Comet}}, unusual proximity between the {{ill|Moon|en|Moon|WD=Q405|lt=moon|1=Moon}} and {{ill|Astronomical object|en|Astronomical object|WD=Q6999|lt=stars|1=Astronomical object}}, close conjunctions of {{ill|Planet|en|Planet|WD=Q634|lt=planets|1=Planet}}, {{ill|red auroras|ja|赤気|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/red auroras|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|halos|ja|光暈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/halos|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暈|1=Halo (optical phenomenon)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q186310}}, and {{ill|white rainbows|ja|白虹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/white rainbows|1=Q11580362|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580362}}.
These phenomena were believed to have profound implications for earthly affairs, especially the fate of states and their rulers (e.g., {{ill|King|en|King|WD=Q12097|lt=monarchs|1=King}}, {{ill|Emperor|en|Emperor|WD=Q39018|lt=emperors|1=Emperor}}, or {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=the Japanese emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}}). Predictable events were avoided on important occasions, while sudden phenomena were observed and interpreted through divination. These interpretations were reported to the ruler, often accompanied by countermeasures. The highest authority on Tenmondō in Japan, the {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, was responsible for formulating these responses, recording the details of the anomalies, and submitting them to the emperor through the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} or {{ill|Kurododokoro|ja|蔵人所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurododokoro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔵人|1=Kurōdo-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6447696}}. This was known as {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}}.
== Tenmondō == == Overview == Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}, Tenmondō, along with Rekidō and {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}}, fell under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. It was overseen by a {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (limited to one person with the Rank of {{ill|Jushichi-i Ge|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushichi-i Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}) and assisted by ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Tenmon phenomena were considered critical to the state's survival, and even Tenmonsei were forbidden to access related texts without authorization ({{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Ritsuryō|1=Yōrō Code}}, Zatsuryō - Hisoryō Genjō clause). Disclosure of observation results was also prohibited.
The Tenmon Hakase, along with the Tenmonsei, conducted nightly observations of the sky to detect anomalies and made Tenmon Missō reports when necessary. They also educated the Tenmonsei using texts such as the {{ill|Book of Han|en|Book of Han|WD=Q846905|lt=Book of Han|1=Book of Han}} and {{ill|Book of Jin|en|Book of Jin|WD=Q1154753|lt=Book of Jin|1=Book of Jin}} (astronomy sections), {{ill|Tengen-sho|ja|天漢書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengen-sho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sangakyohansan|ja|三家薄讃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangakyohansan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
However, compared to Onmyōdō, Tenmondō was not as highly regarded in the Japanese {{ill|Ritsuryō state|ja|律令国家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō state|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 律令制|1=Ritsuryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q742460}}. Observations were often limited to fixed times (around {{ill|the hour of the dog|ja|戌の刻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/the hour of the dog|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戌|1=Dog (zodiac)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q755126}} and {{ill|the hour of the tiger|ja|寅の刻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/the hour of the tiger|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寅|1=yín|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16683683}}).
=== Control by the Abe Clan === During the {{ill|mid-Heian period|ja|平安時代中期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mid-Heian period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the Abe clan gained control of Tenmondō following the succession from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori|1=Kamo no Yasunori}} to {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}}. The discipline became part of the Abe clan's {{ill|family learning|ja|家学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family learning|1=Family knowledge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455404}} (later forming the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}), and participation by outsiders was discouraged.
{{more about Abe clan influence}} === Transition to Modern Astronomy === The transition to modern {{ill|Astronomy|en|Astronomy|WD=Q333|lt=astronomy|1=Astronomy}} began with the establishment of the {{ill|Tenmongata|en|Tenmongata|WD=Q11442660|lt=Tenmonkata|1=Tenmongata}} by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, remnants of Tenmondō's astrological elements persisted.
